facts.text: Founded in 1869 by Alexander Turney Stewart, Garden City was one of America’s first planned communities, complete with wide avenues and a private railroad.
After Stewart’s death, his wife Cornelia erected the Cathedral of the Incarnation, a Gothic Revival landmark that still graces Garden City’s skyline.
The Cradle of Aviation Museum in Garden City showcases Long Island’s pivotal role in early aviation history.
The Waldorf School, opened in 1947, was one of the first Waldorf schools in the United States and remains a notable educational institution in Garden City.
Charles Lindbergh stayed at the Garden City Hotel before his flight, and the hotel was a filming location for the 1930 movie 'The Spirit of St. Louis'.
